Capacity: 788 natural persons and 500 legal persons (in addition to the required engineering and professional training to obtain the authorisation); Compulsory professional training for authorised certifiers every two years

13.759 members of the professional chambers in construction (architectural, construction, electrical engineering and mechanical engineering); 27.320 persons employed in architectural and engineering activities in legal persons (2022); Among active professionals with more than 10 years of experience, existing knowledge and skills show an understanding of nZEB principles in medium-level system design while in execution, design, realisation, quality assurance and performance monitoring, as well as BIM skills is below medium-level; The professional development of authorised engineers is mandatory in the area of monitoring the development of the profession and the acquisition of new knowledge and skills

In 2024, there were 11.600 craftsmen in construction, with 28.378 employees in the first Q4 2025; As of March 2025, there were 21.202 active legal persons in construction with 150.813 employed; The value of works carried out by construction site workers in 2023 is 5.48 billion EUR and is 35.7% higher than in 2022; 13.188 or 62.20% of the enterprises in construction involved in energy renovations are small enterprises with one to nine employees; 5.511 or 26% are enterprises without employees; companies with more than 50 employees account for a total of 1.5% of active legal persons

150.813 employed by legal persons in construction (2024); 28.378 employees working in crafts in construction (2024); a significant reduction in the workforce from Croatia, the region and the surrounding countries, with more and more workers from distant countries; in 2022, 53.772 residence and work permits were issued to foreign construction workers; According to the Croatian Employers' Association (HUP), shortage occupations in construction are: builder, terrain, armourer, concreteist, welder, façade, installer of building elements, construction machine operator, and many others

It is estimated that > 20 ESCO companies are active, although the majority are engaged in projects to reconstruct the ESCO public lighting system; ESCO companies involved in building renovation processes are predominantly oriented towards public buildings (with co-financing provided and often linked to energy suppliers)

MPGI has set up a virtual one-stop-shop for citizens on the portal 'Renovamo.hr', where information is available for several categories of renovation – earthquake renovation, earthquake-damaged real estate renovation and energy renovation; Email addresses and physical addresses are available for one-stop-shop information centres in Zagreb, Sisak, Glina and Petrinji
A total of three registered Energy Communities; Two organised citizens' communities: Criminal Innovation Lab for Climate (CILIC) and Green Energy Cooperative (ZEZ)
